title: lichen_link_tx and lichen_link_rx error codes not documented
description:
lichen_l2.c:209-215 and 577-584: lichen_link_tx() and lichen_link_rx() return negative values on failure, but the error codes are not documented. The L2 layer logs 'Frame build failed: %d' or 'Frame RX failed: %d' without interpreting the code.

What goes wrong: When debugging, users see error numbers like -22 or -74 with no context. They must grep the LICHEN link library source to understand what failed.

Fix: Either (1) document lichen_link_tx/rx return codes in their header, or (2) interpret common errors in the log message (e.g., 'Frame build failed: MIC computation error' instead of just the number).
close_reason: Fixed: Added lichen_link_strerror() helper to interpret error codes. TX/RX log messages now show human-readable descriptions like 'authentication failed' instead of raw numbers like -200.
